title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS company trusted by brands like The New York Times, Wiz, and Lyft, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations reporting to the CRO. The role requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy in high-growth B2B SaaS, with experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR. Candidates need deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, a builder-doer mindset, strong cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR, and experience with globally distributed teams collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment across all dimensions: Head of Revenue Operations role matches target perfectly, 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, B2B SaaS fintech/cloud category is ideal, Salesforce expertise confirmed, and $30M+ ARR scaling experience directly relevant. Tel Aviv/NY hybrid location is in prime acceptable zone.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I noticed Finout is building out Revenue Operations at scale with a globally distributed team anchored in Tel Aviv—exactly the kind of complex infrastructure challenge I've spent 15 years solving in high-growth B2B SaaS. With deep Salesforce expertise and hands-on experience scaling RevOps through the $30M+ ARR inflection point across fintech and cloud-native companies, I'm intrigued by how you're applying cost intelligence to drive revenue efficiency.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong seeks a RevOps Manager to serve as a key partner for Sales and RevOps, providing dashboards, reporting tools, and regional insights to optimize territory performance. Responsibilities include redesigning territory models for global scalability, partnering with Sales leadership and Finance on territory compositions, and maintaining territory mapping in CRM systems like Fullcast and Salesforce. Requires deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models, strong analytical skills (Excel, SQL, BI tools like Tableau), and CRM proficiency. Salary range $149,000–$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's RevOps expertise, B2B SaaS GTM background, and Salesforce/CRM proficiency. Territory strategy and pipeline optimization directly leverage his core competencies, though the role is slightly more specialized in territory modeling than a broader RevOps head position.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years architecting go-to-market operations in B2B SaaS and immediately recognized Gong's territory strategy challenge. Having redesigned territory models and pipeline forecasting systems across multiple scaling stages, I'm excited about the opportunity to bring that operational rigor to Gong's global sales infrastructure and drive the kind of data-driven territory optimization your team is building.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo, an AI research platform used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange, seeks a GTM Engineer who can code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations to make the GTM team more effective. The company is scaling rapidly in 2026 and wants to replace patchwork SaaS tools and manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows — including lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, onboarding flows, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work.
score_reason: Strong alignment on GTM Engineer role, AI-native workflows, and RevOps automation mindset. Slight friction: requires coding ability (not mentioned in candidate profile) and B2B SaaS fit is unclear—Conveo appears to be B2B2C research platform, not traditional enterprise software.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Conveo's vision to replace manual GTM workflows with AI-powered automation—and it immediately resonated with your background building RevOps infrastructure at scale. With 15+ years shipping GTM systems in B2B SaaS, you understand the exact pain points Conveo's team is solving internally. I'd love to explore whether your experience with Salesforce/HubSpot automation and pipeline orchestration could translate to building the purpose-built tools that'll unlock Conveo's 2026 scaling.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Redwood Software, a SaaS-based composable automation platform for ERP, seeks a GTM Engineer to lead the design, implementation, and optimization of AI-driven tools, automations, and processes across marketing. Responsibilities include designing API-driven integrations with the MarTech stack and developing prompt libraries and automation templates. Requires 5+ years in marketing operations or marketing technology with B2B GTM understanding, Python and JavaScript proficiency, API design experience (REST, GraphQL), SQL/JSON/ETL familiarity, and automation framework experience (Make.com, Airflow).
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer role, B2B SaaS expertise, and AI-native automation focus. Primary gap: Omri's background is RevOps/GTM strategy rather than hands-on marketing technology engineering with Python/JavaScript/API design—this is a more technical IC role than his executive profile suggests.
ai_opener: Omri, I noticed Redwood is building AI-driven automation into their GTM stack—a space where RevOps leaders are increasingly expected to bridge strategy and technical implementation. Your 15+ years designing revenue processes in B2B SaaS gives you rare insight into the MarTech pain points Redwood is solving, though this GTM Engineer role has a heavier engineering component (Python, API design, ETL) than typical RevOps positions. Worth a conversation if you're looking to deepen technical chops while leading automation architecture?

title: Global Head of Growth Marketing (B2B SaaS)
description: Glopal, an international cross-border trade platform founded in 2020 with customers in over 100 countries, seeks a Head of Growth Marketing to architect its lead generation engine and pipeline strategy. Requires 5+ years in B2B SaaS marketing with experience building lead gen engines in a scale-up. Candidates need tech literacy with HubSpot and outbound tools (Salesloft, Lemlist, Clay), an ROI mindset measuring qualified pipeline, extreme ownership of the lead number, and fluent English (French a plus).
score_reason: Strong alignment on B2B SaaS, GTM strategy, pipeline metrics, and HubSpot expertise. However, the role is explicitly Growth Marketing-focused rather than RevOps, and Omri's background is stronger in revenue operations and forecasting than demand gen/lead gen engine architecture, which is the primary ask.
ai_opener: Omri here—saw Glopal is scaling cross-border trade across 100+ countries and building out your lead gen engine. With 15+ years architecting GTM motions and pipeline strategy in B2B SaaS (HubSpot, Salesforce, CAC/LTV modeling), I've helped teams go from scattered lead flow to predictable, ROI-tracked pipeline. Happy to explore if you'd benefit from a RevOps lens on your growth infrastructure, or if you want to chat GTM strategy.

title: Marketing Operations Manager
description: Cadonix, a global leader in cloud-delivered ECAD tools, seeks a Marketing Operations Manager to lead the strategy, administration, and optimization of HubSpot as the core marketing and revenue operations platform. The role owns the lead lifecycle, designs lead scoring models, enforces data quality, and builds dashboards for funnel and revenue reporting. Requires 5+ years in Marketing Operations or Revenue Operations in B2B SaaS, deep HubSpot expertise (Marketing Hub required; Sales and Operations Hub preferred), and strong understanding of B2B funnel metrics and GTM motions.
score_reason: Strong alignment with Omri's RevOps core competencies (HubSpot admin, B2B SaaS, pipeline forecasting, lead lifecycle management). Role is slightly marketing-focused versus full RevOps, but the platform strategy and data quality ownership are directly in his wheelhouse.
ai_opener: Omri here—I noticed Cadonix is building serious GTM infrastructure around HubSpot and lead lifecycle optimization. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS revenue engines and deep experience designing lead scoring models and revenue dashboards across Marketing/Sales/Ops hubs, I'd be interested in exploring how to accelerate your funnel metrics and data quality maturity.

title: Senior Marketing Operations Manager
description: Edmentum seeks a Senior Marketing Operations Manager to architect the analytics, systems, and operational frameworks powering its demand and growth engine, and to lead one direct report. Requires strong expertise in lead lifecycle management, funnel analytics, attribution modeling, forecasting, and predictive modeling. Candidates need deep experience with Salesforce, Marketo/HubSpot, and MarTech infrastructure, plus skills in UTM governance, campaign taxonomies, and demand generation mechanics. B2B SaaS or EdTech experience preferred. Offers competitive compensation and benefits.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's core RevOps/GTM expertise in lead lifecycle, attribution modeling, forecasting, and Salesforce/HubSpot stack. Senior-level scope with direct report leadership matches career trajectory; EdTech adds valuable adjacent sector exposure to his B2B SaaS foundation.
ai_opener: Omri has spent 15+ years architecting demand and revenue infrastructure for B2B SaaS companies—the exact operational engine Edmentum is looking to scale. His hands-on expertise in Salesforce, attribution modeling, and pipeline forecasting, combined with leadership experience, directly addresses your need to evolve your analytics and go-to-market frameworks. Given Edmentum's position in EdTech, his proven ability to translate complex funnel mechanics into predictable revenue could be immediately valuable to your growth engine.

title: Sales Operations Manager (Account Management & Expansion)
description: Apollo.io, the leading go-to-market solution for revenue teams trusted by over 500,000 companies and valued at $1.6 billion, seeks a Sales Operations Manager focused on Account Management & Expansion. Founded in 2015 and one of the fastest-growing companies in SaaS (~$250M raised, backed by Sequoia Capital and Bain Capital Ventures), Apollo provides verified contact data for 210M+ B2B contacts. The role supports revenue teams in optimizing account management and expansion processes within a high-growth, unified GTM platform.
score_reason: Strong alignment with RevOps expertise and B2B SaaS pedigree at a unicorn-scale company with proven GTM infrastructure. Role is slightly below target seniority level (Manager vs. Head/Lead), but Apollo's scale, unified platform approach, and account expansion focus directly match core competencies.
ai_opener: Omri, I noticed your RevOps background spans high-growth B2B SaaS—Apollo.io is building the exact infrastructure you've likely optimized for: unified GTM, pipeline forecasting, and revenue team enablement at scale. With your Salesforce/HubSpot expertise and track record in CAC/LTV optimization, you'd be positioned to immediately impact how Apollo's own revenue operations drive account expansion across their 500K+ user base.

title: RevOps Manager
description: Platomics, a healthtech company improving healthcare for millions, seeks a RevOps Manager (part-time, 20h/week with potential to move full-time in early 2027). Responsibilities include administering CRM (HubSpot) and RevOps tools, ensuring data hygiene and integrations, partnering with sales/marketing/finance, and supporting forecasting, territory planning, and quota setting. Requires 3+ years in revenue operations, sales operations, or business analytics, preferably in tech (SaaS, cloud, AI), with knowledge of forecasting methodologies and KPIs like CAC, LTV, and churn. Remote-friendly across Europe.
score_reason: Strong technical RevOps fit with HubSpot expertise, forecasting knowledge, and CAC/LTV proficiency matching core requirements. Slight concerns: healthtech (not SaaS core vertical), part-time structure (may underutilize 15+ years seniority), and Vienna/Europe timezone preference vs. Tel Aviv base.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I saw Platomics is scaling revenue operations in healthtech—a sector where data hygiene and predictive forecasting drive outcomes as much as in SaaS. With 15+ years building RevOps functions around HubSpot, CAC/LTV modeling, and territory planning, I'm intrigued by your 20h/week structure as a potential fit for strategic advisory work, though I'd want to explore whether the scope could expand to maximize impact on your growth engine.
